commit.message:
fix: table floor no longer overrides white wall detection

The table floor protection (Layer 4) was keeping white wall pixels
opaque at the bottom of the image. Now the floor protection is
scaled by inverse wall strength: white wall pixels get no floor
protection, allowing them to be properly removed.

commit.message:
feat: Phase 5 — Firebase Auth, tenant service, CSV/XLSX ingest, SaaS UI

Backend:
- firebase-admin: JWT verification + Firestore tenant metadata
- middleware/auth.js: requireAuth / optionalAuth middleware
- tenantService: ensureDataset, createEnrichedView, logUpload, getHistory
- ingestService: parse CSV/XLSX, normalize postcodes, load to BQ raw_members,
  recreate enriched_gemeente VIEW per tenant
- POST /api/ingest/upload, GET /api/ingest/history
- GET /api/tenant/me, PATCH /api/tenant/me

Frontend:
- firebase + react-router-dom installed
- AuthProvider: Google SSO, onAuthStateChanged, tenant fetch
- ProtectedRoute: redirect to / if unauthenticated
- React Router: / = Landing, /dashboard/* = protected Dashboard
- Landing page: hero + feature cards + Google sign-in CTA
- Dashboard shell: sidebar nav (Kaart, Uploads, Instellingen) + user info
- MapPage: wraps existing MapComponent inside dashboard
- Uploads: drag-and-drop file upload + history table
- Settings: org_name editor

New env vars required (Vercel):
  VITE_FIREBASE_API_KEY, VITE_FIREBASE_AUTH_DOMAIN, VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID,
  VITE_FIREBASE_STORAGE_BUCKET, VITE_FIREBASE_MESSAGING_SENDER_ID, VITE_FIREBASE_APP_ID

Fix eccentricity formula, improve emcee init/progress, add derived posteriors and LaTeX sanitizer

commit.message:
Rebuild leaderboard from all saved results, not just current run

Previously the leaderboard only included models from the current session,
so running model B would overwrite model A's entry. Now all result files
under results/*/*.json are loaded before aggregating scores, giving a
cumulative leaderboard across all runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: reduce extras detection false positives

Filter out Helm-managed resources and Kubernetes auto-created resources
from extras detection, showing skip counters instead of noisy entries.

- Skip HelmRelease inventory items (Layer 1) since Helm-generated
  resources aren't expected to have individual Git source files
- Skip resources with app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=Helm label (Layer 2)
  for resources not tracked in incomplete HelmRelease inventories
- Skip kube-root-ca.crt ConfigMaps and default ServiceAccounts
- Apply IgnoreNamespaces to Layer 2 namespace scanning (fixes
  kube-system being scanned due to cert-manager leaderelection)
- Resolve HelmRelease targetNamespace for managed namespace tracking
- Fix Namespace resources not populating managedNamespaces (cluster-
  scoped resources have empty namespace field)
- Fix parseInventoryID not decoding Flux's colon-to-double-underscore
  encoding in resource names (e.g. system:kube-vip-role)

Architects write verification checks during debate phase

For COMPLEX tasks, each architect now adds domain-specific verification
checks during the debate:
- Frontend: browser checks (pages, elements, interactions)
- Backend: spec checks (files, exports, API contracts)
- Systems: CI checks (build, types, tests, conventions)

Lead compiles these into VERIFICATION_PLAN.md after convergence,
producing a more thorough plan than Lead writing it alone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(beads): ignore br runtime directories in .beads/.gitignore

The br (beads_rust) tool creates two runtime directories inside .beads/:

  .br_history/   — stores command history for interactive br sessions,
                   analogous to shell history files; machine-specific and
                   session-ephemeral
  .br_recovery/  — stores crash recovery state; only relevant while a br
                   command is actively running; should never be committed

These are generated at runtime and vary per machine and session. Committing
them would create noisy, meaningless diffs and pollute the shared history
with local artefacts that have no value to other contributors.

Added to .beads/.gitignore alongside the existing exclusions for SQLite
database files, lock files, and last-touched timestamps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add SVG attractor system to ParticleDots with per-page shapes

ParticleDots now supports SVG path attractors — particles form shapes
and scatter on cursor interaction. New props: svgPath, svgScale,
svgFit, svgAlign, svgOffsetX/Y, svgPoints, svgLinkDistance,
attractStrength. Expertise uses mountain range SVG, case studies uses
WSM logo, contact uses envelope shape. All pages have explicit defaults.

commit.message:
refactor: extract polygraph into separate plugin (#94)

* refactor: extract polygraph into separate plugin

Move all experimental polygraph artifacts (skills, agents) into a
dedicated `artifacts/polygraph/` source tree and generate outputs to
`generated/polygraph/`. Register the polygraph plugin in the Claude
marketplace alongside the main nx plugin.

- Move polygraph skills and agents to `artifacts/polygraph/`
- Remove experimental flag mechanism (`--include-experimental`, meta.json flags)
- Delete `publish-experimental.yml` workflow
- Add polygraph plugin manifest and MCP config in `artifacts/polygraph/claude-config/`
- Refactor `sync-artifacts.mjs` to process both artifact sets via `processArtifacts()`
- Update `bump-version.mjs` to sync version across all plugin manifests
- Update CI workflows to detect polygraph changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: pre-release version for #94

* fix: use relative path source for polygraph plugin in marketplace

The `directory` field doesn't exist on `github` source type.
Use relative path format (`./generated/polygraph`) instead, which
is the correct way to reference plugins in the same repository.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Golf/Tennis scoring: golf/tennis points aggregation system

- Replace per-event 80/50/30/20 scoring with the correct two-layer system:
  golf/tennis points (8/5/3/2/1) accumulate across 4 majors, then a single
  set of Omnifantasy 80/50/30/20 is awarded based on the final ranking
- resultsApi.js: capture ninth_to_sixteenth (position.id 9-16) for golf
  and round_of_sixteen (R16 losers) for tennis; compute rankings[] array
  on each multi-event result fetch
- Add RESULTS_CACHE_VERSION=2 to force-invalidate old per-event cache entries
- points.js: calculatePickPoints uses rankings[] for Golf/Tennis when present;
  falls back to legacy per-event sum for old cache entries without rankings
- filterResultsForLeague recomputes rankings from filtered events
- RulesModal: expand Golf/Tennis/F1 special rules with full detail (golf points
  table, tennis points table, tie-breaking rules); update EP explanation to
  note Golf/Tennis approximation
- Update CLAUDE.md and points tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace clipboard button with native paste event listener

The Clipboard API read() is blocked on iOS Safari. Instead, listen for
native paste events so Ctrl+V / Cmd+V works on desktop and long-press
paste works on mobile.

commit.message:
Complete Gold Tier implementation - All 11 requirements delivered

Major Features:
- Audit logging system with JSONL format and rotating files
- Agent Skills Framework with 23 modular skills across 7 categories
- Ralph Wiggum Loop for autonomous task execution
- Cross-Domain Integration connecting Personal + Business domains
- Odoo 19 ERP integration with JSON-RPC MCP server
- Twitter/X API integration with mention monitoring
- Facebook & Instagram API integration
- Enhanced Gmail and WhatsApp watchers with audit logging
- CEO briefing generator for weekly business reports
- Complete architecture documentation

Components Added:
- audit_logger.py - Centralized audit logging
- agent_skills/ - 23 reusable agent skills
- cross_domain_integration.py - 6 workflows bridging domains
- ralph_wiggum_loop.py - Autonomous agent
- odoo_integration/ - Odoo ERP client and installation
- twitter_integration/ - Twitter API monitor
- social_media_integration/ - Facebook/Instagram integration
- whatsapp_integration/ - WhatsApp monitoring
- skills_cli.py - Command-line interface for skills
- generate_ceo_briefing.py - Weekly business reports
- generate_audit_summary.py - Audit log summaries

Documentation:
- ARCHITECTURE.md - Complete system architecture
- CROSS_DOMAIN_INTEGRATION_DOCS.md - Integration workflows
- ODOO_MCP_SERVER_DOCS.md - Odoo integration guide
- AGENT_SKILLS_DOCS.md - Skills framework documentation
- RALPH_WIGGUM_DOCS.md - Autonomous agent guide
- AUDIT_LOGGING_DOCS.md - Logging system documentation
- Plus 10+ additional setup and integration guides

System Status: Production Ready
All Gold Tier requirements: ✅ Complete

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix TTS voice: use 'Anna from Munich' German female voice

The previous Aria voice ID didn't exist in the account, causing fallback
to browser SpeechSynthesis. Now using a real German professional female
voice from the ElevenLabs library.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Force CSS/JS cache bust via versioned URLs (?v=8)

Old SW was serving stale css/app.css from cache regardless of SW
version bumps. Adding ?v=8 to CSS and JS links in index.html forces
even old SWs to fetch fresh files (cache miss on new URL).
Also: theme-color updated to #ffffff for light mode, sw cache v8.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add gemeente choropleth + segment analysis panel (Phase 4)

- Download PDOK gemeente_2025.geojson (342 features, 187KB) to public/data/
- layerManager: addGemeenteChoropleth() merges penetration_pct into GeoJSON,
  renders fill layer with NVBS colour scale (pale→blue→orange), hover popups
- api.js: getGemeentePenetration(), getNlBaseline(), getSegmentProfile()
- useMap.js: gemeente_choropleth layer loads GeoJSON + API data in parallel,
  visible by default alongside member dots
- SegmentPanel: bottom-right panel with top-10 gemeenten table + CBS index bars
  (audience vs. NL weighted average) for 5 demographic dimensions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix workflow artifact passing between collect and report jobs

The generate-report workflow failed because it tried to download an
artifact named intel-db-{current_run_number}, which never exists since
the collect workflow uploaded with a different run number. Fixed by:

- Using a fixed artifact name "intel-db" with overwrite:true
- Switching to dawidd6/action-download-artifact@v6 for cross-workflow
  artifact downloads
- Adding previous DB download to collect workflow for data continuity
- Adding report type auto-detection from cron schedule
- Adding error handling in report generation
- Fixing requirements.txt path (was backend/requirements.txt)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: ClickUp-style redesign + team messaging + team management

- Design system: ClickUp tokens (shadows over borders, compact spacing, cyan accent #0EA5E9)
- 20+ new cu-* CSS utility classes (tabs, avatars, messages, channels, modals)
- Layout: 240px sidebar with workspace switcher, mobile bottom tabs, breadcrumb
- Team messaging: channels, DMs, threads, reactions, @mentions, search (localStorage)
- Team management: members CRUD, groups, communities, invite/join/leave (localStorage)
- Chat page: dual mode (AI assistants + Team messaging with full channel UI)
- Team page: 5 tabs (AI Team, Members, Groups, Communities, Activity)
- ClickUp design refresh on 40+ client pages (shadow cards, 36px buttons, compact headers)
- Landing page: violet accent → cyan, light sections → #FAFBFC, ClickUp-style CTAs
- Build: 167 pages, 0 new TypeScript errors

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 8 critical bugs across all major features

BUG 1 - Mystery page enhancements
- Enhanced error message visibility (red background, bold text)
- Added button disabled state during loading
- Improved form validation feedback

BUG 2 - Weekend page day filtering
- Added flexible_days parameter (±0-3 days)
- Updated API to support date flexibility in day-of-week matching
- Fixed server-side filtering logic with UTC date parsing

BUG 3 - IATA codes expansion
- Added missing Thai airports: KKC (Khon Kaen), UTH (Udon Thani), BFV (Buriram)
- Added US airports: CLE (Cleveland), RDU (Raleigh)
- getCityName() now supports 150+ airports

BUG 4 - "This Weekend!" badge (verified working)
- Badge only shows for trips 0-7 days away

BUG 5 - Bangkok in popular cities (verified working)
- BKK listed first in popular departure cities

BUG 6 - Weekend URL parameter handling
- Fixed origin parameter lookup to search by code AND city name
- Always converts to IATA code before API calls
- Falls back to JFK if airport not found

BUG 7 - Calendar page functionality
- Added comprehensive form validation
- Fixed error handling and display
- Default month set to current month
- Enhanced API logging for debugging
- Improved error message styling

BUG 8 - Layover page complete rebuild
- Created new /api/layover endpoint for server-side processing
- Reduced from 11+ sequential calls to 1 API call with parallel fetching
- Added DOH (Doha) and KUL (Kuala Lumpur) to hub list
- Prioritized top 5 hubs: SIN, DXB, IST, DOH, KUL
- Added form validation and default date (2 weeks from today)
- Major performance improvement

All features now have proper validation, error handling, and user feedback.

🤖 Generated with Claude Code
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Broadcast email now mirrors in-app notification content automatically

Eliminates the separate email composition workflow (markdown body, subject,
CTA fields). When "Also send email" is toggled on, the email renders the
same title, message, structured sections, banner image, and action button
as the in-app notification. New server-side mini-markup renderer handles
section formatting for email clients. Legacy fallback preserved for any
in-flight scheduled notifications with email_body_markdown.
